name: Page
summary: Class for a single page in a LayOut document.

methods.name: attribute_dictionary
methods.summary: The #attribute_dictionary method returns a copy of the page's attribute dictionary with the given name.
methods.remarks: The #attribute_dictionary method returns a copy of the page's attribute dictionary with the given name.

returns.doc: A copy of the page's attribute dictionary, or nil if there is no attribute dictionary

methods.name: delete_attribute
methods.summary: The #delete_attribute method is used to delete an attribute from a page.
methods.remarks: The #delete_attribute method is used to delete an attribute from a page.

params.name: dictionary_name
params.doc: The name of an attribute dictionary.

methods.name: document
methods.summary: The #document method returns the Document that the Layout::Page belongs to.
methods.remarks: The #document method returns the Document that the Layout::Page belongs to.

methods.name: entities
methods.summary: The #entities method returns all Entitys that are on the Layout::Page.
methods.remarks: The #entities method returns all Entitys that are on the Layout::Page. This is the equivalent of iterating over all LayerInstances and using LayerInstance.entities.

methods.name: get_attribute
methods.summary: The #get_attribute method is used to retrieve the value of an attribute in the page's attribute dictionary.
methods.remarks: The #get_attribute method is used to retrieve the value of an attribute in the page's attribute dictionary. If the third parameter, default_value, is not passed and there is no attribute that matches the given name, it returns nil. If default_value is provided and there is no matching attribute it returns the given value. It does not create an attribute with that name though.

params.name: name
params.doc: The name of an attribute dictionary.

params.name: default_value
params.type: String, Boolean, Integer, Float, Hash, Layout::Dictionary, nil
params.doc: A default value to return if no attribute is found.

returns.type: String, Boolean, Integer, Float, Layout::Dictionary, nil

methods.name: in_presentation?
methods.summary: The #in_presentation? method returns whether the Layout::Page is included in presentations.
methods.remarks: The #in_presentation? method returns whether the Layout::Page is included in presentations.

methods.name: layer_instances
methods.summary: The #layer_instances method returns an array of the LayerInstances for the Layout::Page.
methods.remarks: The #layer_instances method returns an array of the LayerInstances for the Layout::Page.

methods.name: layer_visible?
methods.summary: The #layer_visible? method returns whether a Layer is visible on the Layout::Page.
methods.remarks: The #layer_visible? method returns whether a Layer is visible on the Layout::Page.

throws.when: if layer and page are not in the same document

methods.name: nonshared_entities
methods.summary: The #nonshared_entities method returns the Entities unique to the Layout::Page.
methods.remarks: The #nonshared_entities method returns the Entities unique to the Layout::Page.

methods.name: set_attribute
methods.summary: The #set_attribute method adds an attribute to the page's attribute dictionary.
methods.remarks: The #set_attribute method adds an attribute to the page's attribute dictionary.

params.name: name
params.doc: The name of an attribute dictionary. @param [String] key An attribute key. @param [String, Boolean, Integer, Float, Hash, Layout::Dictionary, nil] value The value for the attribute.

methods.name: set_layer_visibility
methods.summary: The #set_layer_visibility method sets whether a Layer is visible on the Layout::Page.
methods.remarks: The #set_layer_visibility method sets whether a Layer is visible on the Layout::Page.

throws.when: if layer and page are not in the same document
if layer could not be hidden because there must be at least one unlocked, visible layer on each page

properties.summary: The #== method checks to see if the two Layout::Pages are equal.
properties.remarks: The #== method checks to see if the two Layout::Pages are equal. This checks whether the Ruby Objects are pointing to the same internal object.

properties.name: in_presentation
properties.summary: The #in_presentation= method sets whether the Layout::Page is included in presentations.
properties.remarks: The #in_presentation= method sets whether the Layout::Page is included in presentations.

properties.name: name
properties.summary: The #name method returns the name of the Layout::Page.
properties.remarks: The #name method returns the name of the Layout::Page.
